delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
Weekly English group communication/presentation ability using number data/statistics.
Working in groups weekly explaining/presenting ideas related to math.
aims: Have fun improving your English communication/presentation ability using number data/statistics
goals:
Students will be able to convey complex technical data in English using basic information structures.
Students will develop practical communicative presentation competence & learn to express more mathematical/scientific ideas in English.

Students will be able to create English language presentations.
Students will be able to explain/communicate & give SHORT PRESENTATIONS expressing themselves using numbers [technical/mathematical/physics ENGLISH].

Students will be able to have simple, informal conversations which are important to building international relationships.
Students will understand and be able to communicate about numbers and statistics in English.

plan.text:
Introduction to the Class
Written/Spoken Self-introduction using numbers

Time & Frequency
Write/Talk about questions/answers using time & frequency

Numbers & Numerical Facts
Search for Numbers & Numerical Facts on your hometown/prefecture
Small group PRESENTATION on your hometown/prefecture

Arithmetic & Fractions
Write/Ask & Answer original math problem questions

Geometry & Shapes
Write/Talk about various geometric shapes & dimensions

Units of Measurement
Geometric Shapes Field Work: measure & answer questions

Size/Shape & Measurement Description
Write/Describe an object/ sport

Write/Make comparisons about 2 or more things using graph data
Comparisons: general/ number/ percent/ ratio
Search the Internet/newspaper for statistics for final Graph EXAM PRESENTATION

Numbers/Percentages/Ratios
Write sentences use graph data

PIE Graphs
Make an original PIE Graph with EXCEL

Make a BAR Graph
(*Decide EXAM Topic)

LINE Graphs
Read/Put data into LINE Graphs

Trends & Causes
Write/Talk about trends & causes

Written/Spoken Preparation for group EXAM Graph PRESENTATION (25%)
Written/Powerpoint graphic

textbooks: Original class materials will be posted on the KU moodle site [https://moodle.kochi-u.ac.jp/2026/] or handed out in class.
prereq:
Successful completion of basic general education Eikaiwa I & II (speaking/conversation) as a 1st-year student at Kochi University & Science (math/physics) major.
The course is taught in English at a Post Elementary level. It is assumed that students have some basic English communication skills, so students must be ready to try to function in English and improve on their basic communication/presentation skills.
prep:
Students are expected to practice English skills and prepare for all classes.
Practice outside of the classroom is essential.
teachers: ◎ フェビアン ポーラ ダイアン

extra.label: 【テーマ（英語）】(IN ENGLISH)
extra.text: Using English & numbers for math/physics students

extra.label: この授業で身につける「10+1の能力」
extra.text:
専門分野に関する知識
論理的思考力
情報に関するリテラシー
表現力
コミュニケーション力
協働実践力

extra.label:
履修に係わる注意事項
【NOTES ON CLASS ENROLLMENT】
extra.text:
It is very important to ATTEND class regularly (**MAX3 absences), especially the 1st lesson introduction.
Students are expected to ACTIVELY PARTICIPATE in every class.

extra.label: 成績評価に関する補足
extra.text:
55%: 30% In-class Work/Quizzes/Participation/Attitude + 25% Homework/ Preparation
45%: 20% Presentations + 25% Final EXAM Presentation

extra.label: 教員の実務経験の有無
extra.text:
有
教員の実務経験との関連
Have worked as a teacher; teaching in kindergarten, elementary school NPO summer programs, junior & senior high school, vocational schools, cram schools, language schools & university. Before teaching, I worked for a small company in public relations/marketing & advertising. Over the years have volunteered or worked in/with NPO groups.

extra.label: 授業形態
extra.text:
講義形式が中心
口頭での質疑やディスカッションを含む
授業中や授業時間外のグループワークを含む
その他
Some classes will meet online or on demand on moodle
